findings:
## D1: WebGPU in VS Code Webview
VS Code 1.96+ ships Electron with Chromium 128+ — WebGPU (Chrome 113+) is technically present but **disabled by default** in sandboxed/VM contexts (GH issue #235201). On real GPU hardware it is accessible. Transformers.js v3 (`@huggingface/transformers`) supports `device:'webgpu'` in Web Workers — 3-5x faster than WebGL for transformer models. ONNX Runtime Web also offers a WebGPU execution provider. Chrome 145 (Jan 28, 2026) shipped WGSL subgroup_uniformity + synchronous buffer mapping in workers. GraphGPU (github.com/drkameleon/GraphGPU) implements Barnes-Hut force-directed graphs in WebGPU compute shaders. graphrag-rs uses Rust+WASM+WebGPU for knowledge graph queries. Key: always gate on `navigator.gpu` with WebGL/WASM fallback.

## D2: Local LLM Inference on 12GB GPU
Verified Dec 2025-Feb 2026 benchmarks: **Qwen2.5-Coder-7B Q4 (~5GB VRAM)** = best coding model for 12GB GPU (57.9% Aider, 128K ctx). DeepSeek-Coder-V2-Lite (16B MoE, ~10GB VRAM) handles longer context. RTX 5090: 5,841 tok/s on Qwen2.5-7B. 12GB RTX 3060/4070: ~50-80 tok/s single user. Ollama (Jan 2026): OpenAI-compatible API at localhost:11434, CUDA/ROCm/Metal, `ollama-js` npm SDK, Anthropic Messages API compat added Jan 16 2026. Continue extension (VS Code): separate autocomplete + chat models in config.json. vLLM: PagedAttention (50% less fragmentation, 2-4x throughput), full parallel tool calling, access via openai SDK. node-llama-cpp npm: pre-built CUDA/Metal/Vulkan binaries, TypeScript, runs in extension host.

## D3: GPU Agent Orchestration
GPU used at inference/embedding layers only — orchestration runs on CPU. Feb 20 2026 real-world build: RTX 5070 12GB running 3-agent Planner-Coder-Critic system via Ollama (mistral-nemo), ChromaDB + sentence-transformers (all-MiniLM-L6-v2), LangChain+LangGraph, FastAPI+React. VRAM budget for 12GB: Qwen2.5-7B Q4=5GB + embedding model (nomic-embed-text ~500MB, all-MiniLM ~80MB) — comfortably fits. Ollama `/api/embeddings` endpoint enables Node.js-only semantic routing without Python. GraphRAG locally via vLLM+Ollama (chitika.com Jan 2025). GPU usage for agents: inference + embedding generation, no specialized GPU multi-agent scheduling needed.

## D4: ONNX Runtime / Node.js GPU in Extension
ONNX Runtime Web + WebGPU is the cleanest path for webview panels — no native CUDA install needed. `onnxruntime-node-gpu` npm is stale (v1.14.0, 2023). Official `onnxruntime-node` v1.20+ supports CUDA but requires CUDA+cuDNN — too heavy for distribution. Microsoft AI Toolkit extension (Ignite 2025 update) uses ONNX Runtime + DirectML on Windows as reference pattern. **node-llama-cpp**: pre-built CUDA/Vulkan/Metal binaries, TypeScript, auto-detects GPU compute layers — best for embedding inference directly in extension host. Alternative: call Ollama HTTP API (no native bindings). TF.js GPU also requires CUDA install — avoid for distribution.

## D5: GPU Graph Visualization
Sigma.js v3 (WebGL, works in all VS Code webviews today): handles 10K+ nodes, ForceAtlas2 layout, `@react-sigma/core` React wrapper, community detection (Louvain), animated transitions. GraphGPU (github.com/drkameleon/GraphGPU): WebGPU compute shader force-directed layout, Barnes-Hut physics, requires Chrome 113+. Three.js WebGPURenderer: available in r161+, Nov 2025 analysis confirms production viability. Open Vis Summit Dec 2025: a major library reached v9.2 with early WebGPU compute shader support. Recommended: Sigma.js (stable/now) → progressive enhancement to WebGPU layout when `navigator.gpu` confirmed.
recommendations: D1 WebGPU Webview: Gate on navigator.gpu (disabled by default in VS Code). Use Transformers.js v3 WebGPU + WASM fallback in Web Worker. Use GraphGPU Barnes-Hut compute shader for force layout.
D2 12GB GPU LLM: Qwen2.5-Coder-7B Q4 (~5GB) via Ollama + Continue is proven 12GB stack. Use ollama-js SDK. Use vLLM for high-throughput multi-user production serving.
D3 Agent Orchestration: GPU handles inference+embeddings only. VRAM budget: 7B model (5GB) + embedding (0.5GB) fits 12GB. Use Ollama /api/embeddings for Node.js-only semantic routing.
D4 ONNX/Node GPU: Use node-llama-cpp npm (pre-built CUDA/Vulkan/Metal, TypeScript) in extension host. Use ONNX Runtime Web+WebGPU in webview. Avoid onnxruntime-node CUDA.
D5 Spider-Web Viz: Deploy Sigma.js v3+graphology (WebGL, works now in VS Code webview). Progressive enhance to GraphGPU WebGPU compute layout when navigator.gpu is available.
